title: NOC 43109 – Other Instructors | GTR Immigration
description: Other Instructors under NOC 43109 provide specialized instruction outside the traditional classroom setting. This category includes driving instructors, licence examiners, modelling instructors, sewing instructors, and other skill-based trainers. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation typically requires demonstrated expertise in a specific field and provincial licensing in certain cases. These professionals work in private training schools, commercial establishments, and self-employed settings across Canada.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group431 – Assisting occupations in educationMinor Group4310 – Assisting occupations in educationNOC Code43109

duties: Teach motor vehicle operation and road safety
Teach tailoring, dressmaking, and sewing techniques
Provide guidance for fashion shows and advertising
employment_requirements: Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated
Post-secondary education in a related discipline may be required in some cases
Driving instructors must obtain provincial driving instructor licence
Certification by the Canadian Yachting Association may be required for sailing and navigation instructors
Experience in the relevant field is essential
Licensing requirements vary by province and specialty.
salary_prospects: $27,900 – $67,400 per year,depending on specialization, province, and employment type.
Self-employed instructors may earn more depending on business volume.
job_outlook: Growth in vocational and lifestyle training programs
labour_market_demand: Rise in self-employment and vocational skill training
Stable demand in transportation and vocational sectors
Career advancement into senior instructor or examiner roles
Driving instructors and examiners remain particularly important due to commercial transportation growth in Canada.
pathways: Other Instructors fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
Express Entry (if eligible under skilled categories)
